128294.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA strives for an inclusive society. The municipality has a role model function for inclusion and diversity. The municipality fully commits to ensuring that the executive board and the organization truly reflect the population.

128295.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to maintain and strengthen citizen involvement in the coming years. We support a referendum if the dilemma can be captured well in a yes/no question. For other themes, a citizens' assembly might be more appropriate, as was organized recently in Zwolle.

128300.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that bed-bath-bread shelters must continue to exist and preferably be expanded with good guidance. It is inhumane to let undocumented people sleep on the streets; people must receive basic care.

128302.explanation: No one should have to close their windows to breathe. Wood smoke penetrates everywhere in houses, including where children, the elderly, and people with asthma live. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses clean air because health is a right. We are therefore pleased that Zwolle has signed the Clean Air Agreement.

128306.explanation: The noise standards for festivals and events in the municipality of Zwolle are based on nuisance to residents. That is well regulated. Around the city, we want more attention for the negative effects of events on nature.

128307.explanation: Every building should be accessible to people with disabilities. The municipality pays for adjustments needed in municipal buildings. Entrepreneurs pay for adjustments needed in their buildings. The municipality can advise them about this.

128309.explanation: Local, independent media are important in a democracy. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA wants the municipality to financially support the local broadcaster. The municipality does not influence programming or content. However, the local broadcaster must account for expenditures annually.

128314.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA focuses on prevention. BOAs have an important role in this. We want more BOAs and youth outreach workers.

128315.explanation: We deploy cameras temporarily and only if they demonstrably contribute to safety. We always do this with respect for our residents' privacy. Cameras alone do not catch criminals.

128317.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses a real solution: building more affordable owner-occupied and rental homes. It sounds sympathetic to give starters a loan, but in practice, this mainly drives house prices further up. Starters can borrow more, but sellers also ask for more money.

128318.explanation: Everyone seeking housing has the right to a fair chance. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants to build for everyone: for people who have lived in Zwolle longer and for new residents.

128323.explanation: People on welfare often already have a lot on their minds. Guidance, education, and voluntary steps towards paid work work better than imposing obligations. This way, people really get the chance to participate again in society.

128325.explanation: Municipal schemes remain and become available to all people with incomes up to 130 percent of the social assistance standard. This way, we maintain what we already do well and increase other schemes.

128327.explanation: With a basic income, more people receive a stable income and therefore a stable foundation. It also simplifies the implementation of our social policy.

128330.explanation: Parking at shopping centers in the suburbs should remain free as long as no nuisance is experienced. Paid parking is, in our view, a means to combat parking nuisance, not an end in itself.

128332.explanation: Parking regulation, such as permits and paid parking, is a means to reduce nuisance. Introducing a parking permit is not an end in itself for us.

128333.explanation: Care providers should be aware of and take into account the religion, culture, and beliefs of those they care for. Considering all personal wishes is practically impossible.

128338.explanation: Tourists enjoy our city and use the facilities provided by the municipality. Tourist tax helps to fairly distribute the costs of this and invest in the livability and public space of Zwolle.

128339.explanation: Sufficient space has been designated as business parks for new construction. Moreover, there is vacancy in business premises and offices. Therefore, we want to make existing business parks more attractive by making them more sustainable and, for example, transforming them into mixed neighborhoods.

128341.explanation: The right to housing applies to everyone, including refugees. People with a residence permit can only move on from asylum centers if they are offered housing. This reduces pressure on the asylum reception chain.

128345.explanation: Controlled sales lead to fewer street dealers and thus more safety. We therefore advocate for more than five coffee shops in our city, appropriate to the number of Zwolle residents.

128346.explanation: The waiting time for social rental housing in Zwolle is longer than 8 years. Only by consistently ensuring that at least 40 percent of new construction consists of social rental homes can we shorten this waiting time and offer more people affordable rental housing.

128347.explanation: Higher residential flats make it possible to offer more people affordable housing without building over our green space. By building upwards, we preserve space for parks, amenities, and social meeting places. We also build sustainably toward a compact, livable city.

128350.explanation: We want to discourage people from coming to the center by car. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses an accessible center with space for pedestrians, the disabled, cyclists, and public transport. Affordable parking is made possible at the mobility hubs just outside the center.

128352.explanation: We are making the city center low-traffic. This means the center remains accessible to residents and the disabled. We create more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport.

128353.explanation: Separating waste is extremely important for the climate and enabling reuse. Thanks to paying per garbage bag, waste is better separated. This lowers the costs of collecting residual waste, thus reducing waste costs per bag and the waste disposal tax for residents as well.

128354.explanation: A fair should be a celebration for the entire city but not at the expense of livability and safety in the city center. Therefore, Park de Wezenlanden is more suitable for the summer fair.

128357.explanation: An event hall is a commercial activity: the operator must run a profitable business independently. However, the municipality does support and facilitate the arrival of a new event hall because it fits the vibrant and lively city.

128360.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to prevent young people from developing problems. If that fails, appropriate care should be available in their own living environment to support children with what they need. Additional funding for youth care helps shorten waiting lists.

128294.explanation: This is a fine aspiration but not a requirement. Each coalition party must find good candidates themselves.

128295.explanation: Residents must be actively and seriously involved in policy and implementation. That means co-deciding, initiating, and being informed early. There are better instruments for this than a referendum.

128300.explanation: Undocumented migrants receive protection and access to care to prevent exploitation.

128302.explanation: We are not in favor of a ban but want to increase awareness of the harmful effects of wood smoke.

128306.explanation: Recreation and tourism enhance the livability and attractiveness of Zwolle. Restricting noise standards makes the city less attractive for events.

128307.explanation: We invest in making shopping areas accessible for the elderly and people with disabilities.

128309.explanation: Time and good governance are needed within the local broadcaster; ChristenUnie wants to give them the space for this.

128314.explanation: A society where people look out for each other also makes a difference regarding safety. That is why we prefer to focus on community building first. If it turns out that more enforcement officers (e.g., youth officers) are needed alongside this, we can reconsider.

128315.explanation: In some places, this can be used effectively, but increasing camera surveillance is not a goal. A society where people look out for each other also makes a difference in terms of safety.

128317.explanation: A starter loan only causes houses to become more expensive. By building enough affordable homes and ensuring people move on from starter homes to something bigger, we want to offer starters a home.

128318.explanation: Within national frameworks, Zwolle chooses a broad and varied housing supply to ensure movement in the housing market and provide everyone with perspective.

128323.explanation: Everyone can contribute to society in their own way: through volunteering, daily activities, informal care, or paid work. The municipality helps residents with this, also when regular work is not yet achievable. This way, we build an inclusive society where every talent is valued.

128325.explanation: For many arrangements in Zwolle, the current threshold is 130% of the social assistance norm. We want to keep it that way. The law does not allow it to be higher.

128327.explanation: This is not a ChristenUnie position. However, we do want to simplify and make benefits more accessible. Then a basic income is no longer necessary for lower incomes, and higher incomes already manage.

128330.explanation: At shopping centers in neighborhoods such as Stadshagen, Aa-landen, Westenholte, Berkum, and Zwolle-Zuid, parking remains free. In the city center, we want to make parking in garages cheaper than on the street. Paid parking is only introduced where actual problems occur.

128332.explanation: Paid parking is only introduced where actual problems occur. In neighborhoods such as Stadshagen, Aa-landen, Westenholte, Berkum, and a large part of Zwolle-Zuid, parking remains free.

128333.explanation: This is our starting point and also a legal obligation for municipalities. We value it highly. At the same time, we recognize it is not always possible. Within reasonable limits, we want to make this possible.

128338.explanation: In the past period, the tourist tax was actually increased to better align with national prices.

128339.explanation: We invest in attractive and well-accessible business parks with sufficient facilities and space, especially at Hessenpoort and to a limited extent at Marslanden.

128341.explanation: Status holders currently have a priority position under certain conditions, just like other municipality residents. We would like to maintain this to prevent overcrowding at reception locations.

128345.explanation: Alcohol, drugs, and gambling cause a lot of damage. They undermine human dignity and lead to addiction and crime. We combat drug crime, cannabis plantations, and drug labs; an expansion of the number of coffee shops does not fit here.

128346.explanation: We build in all price ranges: 30% affordable, 40% mid-range, and 30% expensive. More than half the homes fall into the affordable segment.

128347.explanation: We build an average of 1,000 homes annually, spread over the city and at its edges: Zwolle-Zuid/South, Vechtpoort, and Stadsbroek. High residential flats are not excluded everywhere.

128350.explanation: Parking in garages and hubs on the edge of the center will be cheaper than on-street parking. This keeps the center a pleasant place to shop and dine, and also allows homes to be built.

128352.explanation: The city center will realistically and step-by-step become low-traffic, while destination traffic remains possible and the residential neighborhoods inside the ring remain easily accessible. This makes the center safer and more attractive for shopping and staying.

128353.explanation: Zwolle is working towards a circular economy where waste no longer exists and raw materials are reused repeatedly. Households that separate waste well and produce little residual waste receive a lower levy. This has prevented a significant increase for households.

128354.explanation: The fair has become too large for the city center and in the past caused hospitality entrepreneurs to have almost no customers during important weeks in the peak season.

128357.explanation: A new event hall is certainly needed, but given the current agreements, there is no reason to assume that extra money is needed for this.

128360.explanation: For serious problems among young people, we offer early and appropriate provisions. Home is the starting point, but safe alternatives are available. If more money is needed for this, we must find it. We invest in the social foundation and families to prevent heavier care.

128294.explanation: Parties decide on the alderman they appoint. Of course, this can also be more women than men. This position would prohibit that.

128295.explanation: The VVD does not need referenda to make decisions. A referendum gives the impression that we are taking a holiday from our responsibility to make decisions. Every day, the VVD listens to what is happening in the city and uses that input to improve proposals.

128300.explanation: If the court has determined that you have no right to asylum, you have no right to stay in Zwolle. If you are here illegally, you must return to your country of origin as soon as possible.

128302.explanation: The municipality has better things to do than fine people who light their open fireplaces.

128306.explanation: In recent years, festivals have been pushed out of the city by unfeasible noise standards. The VVD wants a vibrant city with events.

128307.explanation: Some (monumental) buildings in the city center are only truly accessible with major investments. The municipality can contribute to this.

128309.explanation: The municipality already subsidizes the local broadcaster via national funds. That is enough.

128314.explanation: The VVD wants to invest an additional 1 million euros in safe streets through extra enforcement. For example, by keeping nightlife safe, tackling harassment of women more effectively, and preventing children from being recruited into drug gangs.

128315.explanation: Cameras help keep the city safe. This way we prevent theft, drug dealers, and inappropriate behavior.

128317.explanation: First and foremost, we want to build significantly more. That is the real solution to the housing crisis. In addition, we want to explore how we can give starters more borrowing capacity. For example, through a government fund or a starter loan.

128318.explanation: As a young person from Windesheim or Wijthmen, you should be able to live in your own environment. That is why we support priority access to social rental homes here.

128323.explanation: In social assistance, we always apply a counter-performance in a work-related environment. This keeps people involved in society, even during a period of assistance.

128325.explanation: The best way to escape poverty is a paid job. By applying a higher income limit for poverty schemes, working becomes less rewarding. That is unfair.

128327.explanation: Free money does not exist and a basic income must be financed by taxpayers. We do not find dropping all conditions in social assistance to be supportive and it goes against reciprocity. Also, this makes working less attractive. That is unfair.

128330.explanation: The VVD will not unnecessarily impose huge costs on the residents of Zwolle.

128332.explanation: The current coalition has introduced paid parking in many more neighborhoods. The VVD has already opposed this. That is why we are also against paid parking, for example, in Stadshagen, Berkum, Ittersum, Aa-landen, and Westenholte. This directly affects the wallets of Zwolle residents.

128333.explanation: Good care is important for everyone. Through a personal budget (PGB), you can always choose your own care provider who suits you; we stand for that. But it is unfeasible and unaffordable for the municipality to contract care providers for all religions, cultures, and beliefs.

128338.explanation: With a lower tourist tax, we make Zwolle more attractive to visitors. That is good for the city center, for our entrepreneurs, and for the atmosphere.

128339.explanation: We must invest in our economy and jobs. On industrial sites, the money is earned with which we can pay for our facilities such as healthcare.

128341.explanation: Various priority positions in the social sector are unfair to all other housing seekers. We prohibit priority for status holders. Flexible housing can offer temporary living space for status holders to take the first step out of the refugee center.

128345.explanation: According to the municipality's rules, there is still room for a coffeeshop. We will not limit this further.

128346.explanation: The greatest demand is for houses with gardens for middle incomes. We must build much more of these. Zwolle already exceeds the national standard of 30% social rental housing.

128347.explanation: The VVD chooses to build 1,500 homes per year instead of the 1,000 currently planned. We choose both to build many more houses with gardens and to build more apartments in the existing city.

128350.explanation: Life is already expensive enough. That is why the VVD is against Amsterdam-level parking fees in the city center. We actually lower rates in parking garages on the edge of the center.

128352.explanation: We provide more space for greenery, pedestrians, and terraces. That is why we make Bethlehemkerkplein and Nieuwe Markt vibrant hospitality squares car-free. At the same time, a completely car-free center is harassing residents who sometimes simply need their car.

128353.explanation: In the long term, the VVD supports abolishing pay-per-trash bag, as long as costs for people do not increase.

128354.explanation: We give the fair space and therefore keep it in Park de Wezenlanden. Together with fair operators, we look at attractive decoration and possibilities to increase visitor numbers.

128357.explanation: An event hall is enormously important for the attractiveness of Zwolle. The VVD stands firmly behind the arrival of this great facility.

128360.explanation: In recent years, significantly more money has already been spent on youth care. This has not reduced the problems. That is why we want fewer children in youth care by focusing fully on prevention. Less youth care will then be needed. This way, care remains available for children who really need it.

128294.explanation: Our program emphasizes diversity, inclusiveness, and reliable governance, but no quota for men/women in the college; focus on democratic renewal and rules of the game.

128295.explanation: In our program, we want to involve citizens more often with new forms of democracy (citizens' assemblies, digital participation) and citizens' motions. This supports referenda/more direct input.

128300.explanation: Zwolle continues to humanely accommodate refugees according to the law; integration from day one. Nationally, we strive for faster procedures and quick return to the country of origin.

128302.explanation: D66 wants clean air for everyone. Wood burning causes harmful substances and nuisance. That is why stoves must meet strict EU standards and the municipality must act on complaints. With little wind or mist: no burning and follow the burning guide.

128306.explanation: Established standards and times must be properly enforced by the municipality.

128307.explanation: Accessible public buildings for everyone. Financial aid to entrepreneurs can speed this up.

128309.explanation: The municipality has subsidized the local broadcaster for years (via a grant from the national government). On July 7, 2025, the council and also D66 approved the Council Proposal Advice on the application for designation as local public media broadcaster. The financial frameworks were also established in this.

128314.explanation: To be able to work on safety, it is necessary to invest in prevention. That also means investing in education, youth care, neighborhood programs, and combating poverty.

128315.explanation: The municipality must be cautious here and improve safety without placing cameras everywhere by default. It depends on the location and context. We are not against it, but we are critical.

128317.explanation: D66 Zwolle pays a lot of attention to starters in the housing programming to increase opportunities for first-time buyers.

128318.explanation: D66 wants the voice of the home seeker to be better heard, but is against any form of quota for local residents.

128323.explanation: D66 wants a truly inclusive labor market. It's not that people have a distance to work, but work should have access to people. We look at what is possible and offer support where needed. Employers are crucial in this, and the municipality must assist them with knowledge and expertise.

128325.explanation: In the past period, we agreed to simplify income schemes. There is nothing about the income limit in our program, except that we want to prevent poverty.

128327.explanation: Agreed, there must be room for experiments with basic income/security; this fits with trust and simplifying rules.

128330.explanation: That is against parking policy established by the municipal council, supported by D66.

128332.explanation: Due to the city’s growth and the increase in the number of cars, the streets are becoming increasingly crowded. To better balance public space with interests other than those of motorists, we must handle that space differently.

128333.explanation: This idea conflicts with the fact that demand for care is increasing sharply while the number of available care providers is decreasing.

128338.explanation: In 2023, D66 agreed to an increase in the tourist tax.

128339.explanation: In Zwolle, we are careful with space; we prioritize sustainability and clustering of businesses; expansion of business parks is only allowed in a targeted manner.

128341.explanation: Explicitly: newcomers are a sustainable part of the city's growth; priority for status holders accelerates their integration. They can then reshape their lives and contribute to society.

128345.explanation: Zwolle is currently below the nationally ratio-established number of shops.

128346.explanation: D66 wants to build more for seniors, starters (small and especially affordable homes), and students. This can also encourage mobility. The fixed division of cheap, mid-range, and expensive housing can be applied more flexibly to accelerate production.

128347.explanation: We want smart densification; taller residential buildings where appropriate. For example, D66 supports the principles for Kop van Oost.

128350.explanation: From the mobility strategy, ask for a fair contribution, which is more expensive in the center. Cheaper parking works counterproductively.

128352.explanation: A low-car/car-free center fits with prioritizing pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport.

128353.explanation: Disagree, pay-as-you-throw works. We separate more, have less waste and more raw materials, and see a positive trend in how we handle waste.

128354.explanation: The size of the funfair no longer fits in the city center. But whether the Wezenlandenpark is suitable must be proven.

128357.explanation: D66 wants to increase the budget for culture and events by €2 million in the coming period and also let the budget grow with the city’s growth. This also gives an event hall a better foundation.

128360.explanation: We want more money for youth care for quality, prevention, and supporting the chains well.

128294.explanation: A balanced male-female distribution in the executive board is good and can contribute to different perspectives. At the same time, the board’s composition should primarily be based on qualities, experience, and suitability.

128295.explanation: Involving residents in important choices is necessary and valuable. However, the responsibility for decision-making lies solely with the elected representatives. Resident participation may and must be stronger, but the CDA prefers other forms of participation.

128300.explanation: Zwolle must accommodate asylum seekers but does not need to organize shelter for people who are not entitled to it.

128302.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain possible. At the same time, it is important to limit nuisance and health risks. Where there is disturbance to neighbors, the municipality must be able to intervene and enforce.

128306.explanation: We must be careful with our hearing. By applying stricter noise standards at festivals and parties, we protect visitors and neighbors from hearing damage and nuisance, while still allowing events.

128307.explanation: The CDA considers it important that buildings in Zwolle are accessible. The municipality can support entrepreneurs by helping think about practical solutions to make buildings more accessible for people with disabilities. Together, we create a city where everyone can participate.

128309.explanation: The local broadcaster plays an important role but must be able to work independently. Money from the municipality can put that independence under pressure. Therefore, additional municipal funding is not desirable.

128314.explanation: The CDA wants to invest in safety in Zwolle. That means sufficient neighborhood police officers and enforcement officers. Rules must be complied with and enforced for a safe and fair society.

128315.explanation: The CDA wants everyone to feel safe and be safe in public spaces, during the day and evening. Camera surveillance remains a tool used where needed, as part of a broader approach with lighting, supervision, and safe routes.

128317.explanation: The CDA wants to give starters more opportunities in the housing market. We are positive about the idea of a starter’s loan and think the municipality should investigate if this can be used in Zwolle.

128318.explanation: The CDA believes people should have the chance to build their housing career in their own city. Therefore, it is good to give Zwolle residents priority in the allocation of social rental homes so they can stay in their own environment.

128323.explanation: Everyone can and may participate in Zwolle. For those who can, the municipality also asks to participate in the labor market, or as a volunteer if working is (not yet) possible.

128325.explanation: The CDA wants to maintain the current generous poverty policy. We find it important that poverty schemes are simple, quick, and accessible, so residents who need help can use it without obstacles.

128327.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a basic income. Work and participation in society come first.

128330.explanation: The CDA considers it important that local shopping centers remain easily accessible and reachable for everyone. If there are no parking problems, paid parking is not necessary.

128332.explanation: In neighborhoods like Stadshagen, Aa-landen, Berkum, Westenholte, and large parts of Zwolle South, there is currently no reason for paid parking. The CDA is therefore against introducing paid parking in these neighborhoods.

128333.explanation: The CDA believes care should be personal. Freedom of choice for a care provider that matches someone’s religion, culture, or beliefs is part of that.

128338.explanation: The CDA feels the current tourist tax is appropriate. Visitors thus contribute to facilities in the city.

128339.explanation: The CDA believes that Zwolle should be able to provide space for companies that fit here, such as startups and sustainable entrepreneurs. Expansion of business parks is possible, preferably at the edges of the city, with attention to accessibility and good public transport connections.

128341.explanation: The CDA wants to continue building sufficient affordable rental homes for all citizens of Zwolle. However, priority for status holders with a residence permit is necessary to prevent the asylum chain from getting stuck.

128345.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the number of coffeeshops to a maximum of four and does not want additional coffeeshops. If there is structural nuisance, the coffeeshop must be closed. Within a 500-meter radius around schools, we will tighten supervision on dealing.

128346.explanation: The CDA wants at least 30% of new-build homes to be social rental housing. This aligns with national guidelines. More social rental housing must be built, but we must also work on building mid-range rental homes and affordable owner-occupied homes.

128347.explanation: In Zwolle, the maximum building height per part of the city has been established. Where higher construction fits within those frameworks, that is perfectly fine. At the same time, each location should be assessed for what suits its environment.

128350.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce parking pressure in the city center. Making paid parking cheaper is not part of that. The CDA does want to maintain space for those who really need to come by car, such as the disabled and caregivers.

128352.explanation: The center must remain accessible and reachable for the disabled, caregivers, and people dependent on a car.

128353.explanation: The CDA wants to work together to reduce waste per inhabitant. The current system of paying per garbage bag encourages waste separation and generally results in lower costs. Therefore, we are not in favor of abolishing pay-per-bag.

128354.explanation: The city center is very compact and due to redesigning it as a residential area, there is increasingly less space for larger attractions. The current fair is also gaining better shape and attendance; relocating it to the city center is therefore unnecessary for CDA Zwolle.

128357.explanation: The CDA believes that the new events hall is important for Zwolle. Additional investment can contribute to a good, future-proof facility for events.

128360.explanation: The CDA believes that good youth care must always be available for those who need it. This sometimes requires extra effort, but we must also ensure that youth care remains affordable and manageable.

128294.explanation: We do not practice positive discrimination: it is about the quality of the aldermen and not whether they are men or women.

128295.explanation: For issues that have major consequences for Zwolle, a citizens' consultation takes place and its outcomes are fully incorporated into decision-making.

128300.explanation: The sheltering of rejected asylum seekers should not be the responsibility of municipalities. The asylum problem is the result of years of failing asylum policy and should not be offloaded onto municipalities.

128302.explanation: There is already enough symbolic politics in Zwolle.

128306.explanation: Zwolle should be vibrant, and festivals and parties are part of that. There should be clear start and end times so everyone knows what to expect.

128307.explanation: We find the term 'accessible' strange. You are either accessible or not. Or somewhat, and then as an entrepreneur, for example, you are wheelchair-friendly.

128309.explanation: The local broadcaster receives enough money from the national government. It is unnecessary for the municipality to provide financial support.

128314.explanation: Swollwacht also advocates for enforcement officers with local knowledge (fixed neighborhoods so they know residents and can intervene more quickly).

128315.explanation: More camera surveillance and better lighting as well. Safety takes precedence over privacy.

128317.explanation: We want to help residents who can pay high rent but cannot get a mortgage, for example with a municipal guarantee. A starter loan is also a good tool.

128318.explanation: Residents of Zwolle come first when it comes to housing.

128323.explanation: Not everyone receiving welfare is able to work. Moreover, there is a risk of displacement in the labor market. Make socially meaningful work a paid job. That is the best way out of welfare.

128325.explanation: Poverty is not a choice and has far-reaching consequences for someone's life. Better guidance from welfare to work is important, and work must pay. But help with poverty must always be accessible.

128327.explanation: A basic income does not mean that someone is financially independent. It is better to invest in financial independence and better guidance toward paid work.

128330.explanation: For many people, shopping by car is not a luxury but a necessity. If they also have to pay extra for this, you make it unnecessarily difficult for our residents.

128332.explanation: Parking must never become a cash cow for the municipality. Residents should not be punished for the fact that they have (or need) a car.

128333.explanation: Given the increasing waiting lists in healthcare and the many people who rely on care, it is already great if that care is provided well and on time.

128338.explanation: It should not become unattractive to come to Zwolle.

128339.explanation: With due consideration for careful use of space.

128341.explanation: Priority for status holders does not do justice to home seekers who have been on the waiting list for years. Every home seeker should have the same opportunities.

128345.explanation: The municipality does not need to facilitate drug use.

128346.explanation: 30% social rental - 40% mid-range - and 30% expensive. This stable line provides certainty and prevents constantly changing policies. In the long term, Swollwacht wants to increase the share of social rental because waiting times keep rising.

128347.explanation: No absurd high-rise buildings that undermine quality of life, no Manhattan along the canals of Zwolle. So many more people in the same public space will cause major problems. Housing must be balanced and contribute to livability. Livability must not be undermined.

128350.explanation: Visitors will avoid Zwolle's city center if this parking policy continues. That has major consequences for entrepreneurial Zwolle.

128352.explanation: Car-free zones are fine to some extent, but everyone must be able to reach the city center by car.

128353.explanation: Diftar must be abolished because it is complex, socially unjust, causes frustration instead of awareness, and encourages abuse. Single-person households are disproportionately burdened.

128354.explanation: The summer fair belongs in the city center and nowhere else. It should simply be allowed ten days per year.

128357.explanation: Zwolle deserves a magnificent events hall that surpasses the old IJsselhallen. That is good for the city and therefore good for our residents.

128360.explanation: The waiting lists in youth care are alarming. That justifies larger investments in youth care.

128294.explanation: For the lives of Zwolle residents, it is more important which parties will be in charge soon. A good gender balance is of course always preferred.

128295.explanation: Referenda give ordinary people direct influence on policy. This has a positive impact on decision-making by our executive board of Mayor and Aldermen because they can always be corrected when making ill-considered or inexplicable decisions.

128300.explanation: We stand in solidarity with people who have to flee war, violence, and oppression. That solidarity must be well organized. We choose small-scale solutions and spread people as much as possible across the city. We will not solve everything within Zwolle.

128302.explanation: We fully commit to improving air quality. Among other things through the clean air agreement. It is not necessary to ban wood burning. However, we will consider regulations such as the burning alarm.

128306.explanation: We invest in lively festivals, especially those that young people need. This enriches the city and its cultural offerings. We live in a city, and not every sound is immediately considered a nuisance.

128307.explanation: The municipality must fully focus on improving accessibility. By supporting (sports) clubs and appointing an accessibility mayor who can help entrepreneurs with practical tips. Finances are primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves.

128309.explanation: Zwolle deserves a good local broadcaster. Unfortunately, there have been various problems with this for years. More money is not the solution. Until there is a proven stable and qualitatively good broadcaster, the municipality should definitely not allocate extra funds.

128314.explanation: We invest specifically in youth workers and community workers. They strengthen social connections and community spirit in our neighborhoods. This prevents other costs, for example in care or police deployment.

128315.explanation: More cameras may be necessary if the situation demands it. However, this must be temporary and demonstrably effective.

128317.explanation: The starter loan only drives housing prices even higher while costing a lot of money. The SP prefers to invest that money in building affordable homes.

128318.explanation: The world does not end at Hattem. Most people who buy or rent a house in Zwolle already come from Zwolle. If every municipality did this, the Netherlands would come to a standstill.

128323.explanation: We wholeheartedly support volunteer work. At the same time, we must be vigilant that volunteer work is truly voluntary and that regular work is rewarded with a salary. We will immediately stop the mandatory 'counter-performance' in social assistance and guarantee people work.

128325.explanation: Thanks to years of SP efforts, many poverty schemes have been raised to a threshold of 130% of the social minimum. As a result, we offer more and more people security. We will extend this approach to all schemes.

128327.explanation: With plans to help people into work, the basic job is also introduced. Good guidance, training, and working towards sustainable (permanent) jobs are the basis for assistance.

128330.explanation: There must be sufficient support for expanding paid parking, and the support measurement will be reintroduced. There is no reason to introduce paid parking at shopping centers now.

128332.explanation: There must be sufficient support for expanding paid parking, and the support measurement will be reintroduced.

128333.explanation: For many care tasks this is not relevant. It is important that care workers do their work professionally and with passion.

128338.explanation: Visitors may be asked for a contribution to enjoy all the beauty Zwolle has to offer. Our city attracts people through its atmosphere, hospitality, and activities-not because the tourist tax is a few cents higher or lower.

128339.explanation: If there is sufficient demand, there is extra space at Hessenpoort.

128341.explanation: The SP wants to introduce an urgency scheme. The urgency of the home seeker should be decisive, not the status. Status holders qualify for this as they have an urgent housing need.

128345.explanation: The city is growing and there is room for an additional coffeeshop. The SP’s starting point for coffeeshop policy is public health and prevention-not moralizing and repression.

128346.explanation: The waiting list for a social rental home was 4.6 years in 2019 and has risen to almost 9 years in 2025. The SP therefore places the right to housing central, primarily by quickly building many extra social rental homes.

128347.explanation: High-rise buildings can be allowed where appropriate.

128350.explanation: Current visitor rates are not a major barrier to visiting Zwolle. We would rather lower the costs for permit holders in neighborhoods with paid parking.

128352.explanation: We work towards a further car-free city center. However, it will remain easily accessible for less able people, both by car and public transport.

128353.explanation: During the last coalition period, our city has become much dirtier-a direct consequence of a disastrous waste policy. The so-called diftar system was introduced. The SP wants to abolish this and aims for a collective, solidary, and simple system. This is how we will clean up our city again.

128354.explanation: Extra funds are now allocated and plans implemented to make the fair at its new location more attractive; we support this. If after a few years this proves insufficient and there is broad support to move the fair back, we will support that too.

128357.explanation: A new event hall is extremely important for the city. The SP wants to finally speed up the process so Zwolle quickly has a future-proof hall fitting the city.

128360.explanation: Good youth care that helps. We all need help sometimes in life. We must be ready for young people with good youth care you can count on. That is why we invest in youth care and make it free from waiting lists, market forces, and bureaucracy.

128294.explanation: Women are still underrepresented, including in politics. It is important that politics is a good reflection of society.

128295.explanation: The advisory referendum will be given a role in important decisions. A citizens’ assembly is also a good means to involve residents.

128300.explanation: Zwolle will be a place where all people, regardless of residence status or documents, have the right to humane treatment, safe housing, protection against premature or unlawful evictions, and against policies that violate human rights.

128302.explanation: We will restrict wood burning. Wood burning causes a lot of fine particles and health problems for residents.

128306.explanation: There are already clear standards, but residents still regularly experience nuisance. The current rules are insufficient, so we need to look at how to adjust them.

128307.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Where entrepreneurs need help making their buildings accessible, it is important the municipality supports them.

128309.explanation: Strong, independent, local media and regional broadcasters are very important for Zwolle’s residents. There will be no budget cuts here.

128314.explanation: We want to invest in more enforcement to combat nuisance, environmental crimes, animal abuse, and illegal dumping.

128315.explanation: Camera surveillance is used very cautiously due to privacy concerns. Camera surveillance offers only an illusion of security and often merely displaces the problem. Automatic facial recognition is too intrusive and will not be used.

128317.explanation: A nice idea, but this merely shifts the problem. More affordable housing is needed.

128318.explanation: It is good if part of the homes is allocated to home seekers with a local connection.

128323.explanation: The municipality supports benefit recipients to engage in volunteer work, informal care, and internships, but we do not require a counter-performance from people entitled to social assistance.

128325.explanation: The minimum income policy has been extended to 130% of the social minimum in Zwolle. If this proves insufficient, the Party for the Animals wants to increase this percentage.

128327.explanation: A basic income can promote more equality. Therefore, we want the municipality to focus on a pilot with a basic income.

128330.explanation: Parking is not currently free at every shopping center: payment is already required for shopping centers around the city center. Cars occupy a disproportionate amount of public space. Therefore, we do not want to encourage car use, while considering people who genuinely depend on cars.

128332.explanation: Cars take up a disproportionate amount of public space and are stationary 95% of the time. This space cannot then be used for greenery or for children to play. Therefore, we do not want to encourage car use.

128333.explanation: Many people appreciate receiving care from someone who shares their culture and values. In times of aging and shortages in care, this may not always be possible. Where possible, the municipality supports residents in finding a suitable care provider.

128338.explanation: Tourism often causes damage to the climate and nature due to accompanying polluting transport and overconsumption. Therefore, the municipality uses the proceeds from the tourist tax to promote sustainability and strengthen nature to limit tourism’s damage.

128339.explanation: Zwolle only focuses on companies (and sites) that contribute to a sustainable society and have future prospects. Much more efficient use of scarce space is necessary. There will be no data center in Zwolle.

128341.explanation: Status holders are lawfully in the Netherlands and are therefore allowed to stay. This means they must build a life here, beginning with housing. It is not good to remain in asylum accommodation for a long time. Giving priority only leads to about a month’s extra waiting time for other housing seekers.

128345.explanation: It has been agreed in Zwolle that a maximum of five coffee shops are allowed. This means there is room for one more. In our view, this one should not be located near schools.

128346.explanation: The current housing crisis results from cutbacks in the social housing sector. Therefore, Zwolle prioritizes building affordable homes: at least 40% of new homes should be social rental, as there is a great shortage. Additionally, Zwolle focuses on building affordable owner-occupied homes.

128347.explanation: More high-rise buildings are allowed, provided sufficient greenery is added and it fits with the surroundings.

128350.explanation: Parking should take place as much as possible on the edge of the center and, in the future, in mobility hubs. Parking there will be cheaper.

128352.explanation: Where possible, Zwolle chooses a car-free city center.

128353.explanation: Zwolle separates waste better with this system than before. Burning residual waste is very costly and bad for the environment. Proper separation of waste and raw materials is cheaper for residents.

128354.explanation: The fair is a fun event for Zwolle. However, to prevent nuisance for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors to the city center, the fair fits better in Park de Wezenlanden. The municipality applies strict rules to prevent noise nuisance for animals and residents.

128357.explanation: The events hall will be privatized. It is unnecessary for the municipality to contribute to this.

128360.explanation: It is important that more money goes to youth care when needed. We should primarily invest in prevention by strengthening the social foundation in Zwolle.

128294.explanation: A council with an equal distribution between men and women is better balanced and demonstrably makes better decisions. It increases recognition and trust among residents. Diversity is not a goal in itself but strengthens quality and governance capacity.

128295.explanation: Important decisions require more than a simple yes/no choice. We opt for citizen assemblies, where residents together with experts explore, weigh, and prioritize topics, so that the council can make better-informed decisions.

128300.explanation: No one should end up on the street. A modest, dignified shelter with guidance and care prevents nuisance and human suffering, and offers opportunities for return or onward steps where possible.

128302.explanation: Wood burning causes a lot of fine particles and worsens air quality. We want to prioritize health with stricter rules, discouragement, and eventually phasing out in densely built areas.

128306.explanation: Zwolle may be vibrant. Instead of generic stricter standards, we choose tailored solutions, clear agreements, and good enforcement.

128307.explanation: Accessibility is a basic requirement. We help entrepreneurs with advice and co-financing to remove barriers, so that everyone can participate.

128309.explanation: Strong local journalism is important for democracy. We support local media so that residents remain well informed.

128314.explanation: Safety requires visible presence in neighborhoods. We want sufficient community enforcement officers who work neighborhood-focused and are well trained in de-escalation, so safety increases without unnecessary repression.

128315.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety but is not a cure-all. We only deploy it in a targeted and temporary manner, with attention to privacy and always combined with other measures.

128317.explanation: Starters have a hard time in the housing market. With tools such as starter loans, we increase their chances, combined with more affordable homes and appropriate new construction.

128318.explanation: Housing is a basic right. We want to allocate based on need and urgency, not origin. This prevents division and ensures transparent, legally sustainable rules.

128323.explanation: Mandatory work often leads to stress and mistrust. We choose guidance, trust, and removing barriers so that people who can and want to contribute can do so sustainably.

128325.explanation: Many low-income workers fall outside provisions. By raising and indexing income limits, we make support fairer and available to a larger group in need, preventing 'no money before month-end'.

128327.explanation: The current system of benefits and controls does not work, costs too many people (while agencies already have staffing problems) and money. A trial with basic income or similar forms provides security, trust, and participation. Our residents deserve that.

128330.explanation: Accessibility for shops is important. We opt for tailored solutions per location, with short-term parking where needed and good alternatives by bike and public transport.

128332.explanation: We prefer smart solutions such as mobility hubs and shared mobility, and only introduce permits where they demonstrably help.

128333.explanation: Good care is appropriate care. Where possible, people should be able to choose a care provider that matches their language, culture, or beliefs, as long as quality and accessibility are guaranteed.

128338.explanation: Tourist tax helps fairly finance the pressure on the city. We must always stay in contact with our hospitality and arts and culture institutions. We keep the rate reasonable and use the proceeds visibly for livability and facilities to improve the city.

128339.explanation: Space for work remains necessary. Expansion can be sustainable, well accessible, and only after existing sites are better utilized and made more sustainable.

128341.explanation: We allocate homes based on urgency and need, not labels. Rapid integration mainly requires more homes and smart housing forms.

128345.explanation: A regulated supply is better than a ban. With strict conditions, distance from schools, and good enforcement, we maintain control and limit nuisance.

128346.explanation: A fixed percentage does not suit every neighborhood. We focus on affordability and a good mix of homes, fitting the neighborhood and allowing room for progression.

128347.explanation: High-rise buildings can contribute to housing construction, provided they are of good quality: sufficient greenery, facilities, good mobility, and support from local residents.

128350.explanation: Space in the city center is scarce. We seek a balance between accessibility and livability and invest in public transport, bicycles, and mobility hubs.

128352.explanation: We are working towards a car-free city center: cleaner, safer, and more attractive. We will do this step by step, with good alternatives for everyone.

128353.explanation: We want waste charges to depend on how much waste you produce, not on the number of bags you use. With a fixed amount for everyone and extra protection for people with low incomes or medical waste, the system remains fair and understandable.

128354.explanation: The city center must remain livable. We choose a location that can better handle safety, accessibility, and nuisance.

128357.explanation: A new event hall is important for Zwolle and the region. We want swift clarity about location and design, with attention to accessibility.

128360.explanation: Shortages in youth care affect children and families. We invest in quality, prevention, and better cooperation.

128294.explanation: The quality of administrators must be paramount.

128295.explanation: Yes, this is a way to involve residents more in decision-making. By the way, we also support directly elected mayors.

128300.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers can be temporarily accommodated while efforts are made for their return to their country of origin.

128302.explanation: Only burn wood when it is responsible according to the Burning Guide (air, wind). Reduce complaints about wood smoke (stoves) in consultation; possibly involve neighborhood mediation, and enforce if well-founded complaints exist.

128306.explanation: The focus is mainly on enforcing existing noise standards.

128307.explanation: This is primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs. The municipality can provide advice.

128309.explanation: The local broadcaster is important, but no extra funding needs to be made available.

128314.explanation: Safety and the feeling of safety are under pressure.

128315.explanation: Camera surveillance should be used more at locations with serious nuisance and/or where many people gather in public spaces (prevention). This does not mean cameras everywhere; privacy is also important.

128317.explanation: A starter loan is not a municipal responsibility.

128318.explanation: Zwolle is primarily responsible for allocating social rental homes to those in Zwolle seeking such housing.

128323.explanation: Receiving social assistance benefits may require a social counterpart. This helps people stay healthy and increases job prospects. Exceptions allowed, e.g., in cases of informal care or long-term illness.

128325.explanation: This income limit is already high in Zwolle.

128327.explanation: Basic income is national policy. It is unwise for a municipality to develop independent policies in this area.

128330.explanation: Where it is currently free, it can remain free. Residents and visitors should not face unnecessary costs.

128332.explanation: Changes to paid parking zones and permit systems only after consultation with residents, institutions, and businesses. No forced expansion of paid parking zones where residents need a permit.

128333.explanation: If possible, this can be taken into account, but it must not be a requirement.

128338.explanation: The tourist tax rate in Zwolle is reasonable.

128339.explanation: Zwolle has reserved sufficient space for businesses for more than 10 years. Furthermore, existing business parks have room for expansion.

128341.explanation: Equal treatment of status holders when allocating rental homes; no priority policy based on group.

128345.explanation: No expansion beyond the current four coffeeshops and good supervision of existing coffeeshops (age of visitors, nuisance, street vending nearby). The negative health effects of soft drugs are underestimated, as are their addictive nature and social impact.

128346.explanation: Stadspartij Zwolle believes the percentage of social rental housing should match demand as much as possible, now and in the future.

128347.explanation: Only in situations where this is possible in good consultation with local residents. This way, the demand from specific groups can be met.

128352.explanation: Exceptions for residents, people with disabilities, market traders, and entrepreneurs. Essentially, this is about a low-traffic center with car-free squares. This is important for the living environment, quiet shopping, relaxed nightlife, and providing sufficient space for cyclists and pedestrians.

128353.explanation: The pay-as-you-throw system must be thoroughly evaluated: costs, rates, effectiveness, reuse, litter. This will lead to abolishing payment per waste bag and making underground container openings larger.

128354.explanation: The Zwolle fair creates a pleasant atmosphere in the city center. For city center residents and hospitality businesses, the fair was a given. Park de Wezenlanden is not a good location for the fair organizers, residents, or nature. Also, this large park adds little atmosphere to the fair.

128357.explanation: A new event hall as a successor to the IJsselhallen should have existed long ago. There is great demand for it-for concerts, congresses, parties, corporate events, markets, and fairs.

128360.explanation: This is less a matter of money and more about waiting lists and lack of specialized staff. Prevention must also be strengthened. Moreover, youth care should not have been decentralized, as the service now differs per municipality and there is often insufficient expertise.

128294.explanation: LEF wants more diversity, but we should not see achieving gender quotas as the ultimate goal. We believe it is a good first step, but more is needed for representation of every resident.

128295.explanation: LEF wants to give citizens a bigger voice in politics. They should have the option to express their opinion and the assurance that their views are listened to.

128300.explanation: LEF wants to provide shelter for those who need it. The alternative is that asylum seekers become homeless, which only complicates contact and support for their return to their home country.

128302.explanation: Wood stoves should be banned to improve overall air quality. LEF wants to explore the possibility of subsidies to replace wood stoves with green heating sources.

128306.explanation: The noise standards are set so that they are not harmful and correspond to what you encounter in daily life. Through smart design and collaboration with residents, better solutions can be found.

128307.explanation: Public spaces and buildings in Zwolle must be accessible to everyone, regardless of their mobility.

128309.explanation: Reliable and independent journalism is crucial for democracy. We want to promote this. We also wish to safeguard the quality of public broadcasting.

128314.explanation: More enforcers do not address the core problem behind insecurity. LEF prefers to invest in equal opportunities, prevention, and social neighborhoods.

128315.explanation: Surveillance cameras and their underlying systems are expensive and do not necessarily provide more security. They mainly offer visibility. Additionally, the citizens of Zwolle have a right to privacy.

128317.explanation: While a starter loan does make it easier for first-time buyers to get a home, they are subsequently at a disadvantage due to the extra costs. LEF wants to explore other options to support starters and youth so they are not disadvantaged afterward.

128318.explanation: Priority should be given based on your situation and the urgency of your need for housing. Situations like homelessness constitute a valid reason for priority.

128323.explanation: They usually already do this, and LEF also believes that the right to a livable income should not depend on what someone does or does not do.

128325.explanation: LEF supports livelihood security. More assistance should be provided for people with low incomes to give them room and ensure that people do not fall just outside all schemes.

128327.explanation: LEF supports a basic income so that everyone has livelihood security.

128330.explanation: LEF wants to make public transport within Zwolle as attractive as possible. For shopping centers, this is not always realistic, for example when picking up larger and heavier items. Therefore, LEF wants to make parking spaces at shopping centers a blue zone.

128332.explanation: By introducing parking permits, we can ensure there is enough space for residents to park their cars. Furthermore, LEF wants to invest in good and affordable public transport and park-and-ride zones to reduce the number of cars in neighborhoods and the city center.

128333.explanation: People must have the freedom to choose where and from whom they want to receive their care.

128338.explanation: We welcome tourists in Zwolle, but it does mean extra maintenance for our city. Through a tourist tax, they contribute to this. LEF believes the current tourist tax is reasonably affordable and does not need to be cheaper.

128339.explanation: LEF wants to focus on sustainability and reducing consumption, potentially making the expansion of business parks unnecessary.

128341.explanation: LEF believes all migrants and Dutch citizens have the right to the same facilities. Everyone deserves housing, and everyone who needs it should have priority.

128345.explanation: People should be able to buy cannabis freely. If there is demand for a new coffee shop, the municipality should not stand in the way.

128346.explanation: It is difficult for young people to get housing because older generations have more capital to buy a home. By focusing on social rental housing, everyone can get a home and we reduce inequality of opportunity. Additionally, this discourages unscrupulous landlords.

128347.explanation: Apartment complexes use land efficiently, but high-rise buildings affect the living environment and quality. LEF prefers apartment complexes with a maximum of four or five floors to strike a balance.

128350.explanation: The city center must become more accessible without a car. LEF wants to invest in good and affordable public transport, with park-and-ride locations around the city for cars. Additionally, LEF wants to invest in quality bicycle parking to make cycling more attractive.

128352.explanation: At the moment, it is not realistic to make Zwolle's city center car-free. LEF wants to focus on accessible and affordable public transport. This will minimize cars in the center while keeping the city accessible to everyone.

128353.explanation: Paying per garbage bag can have a counterproductive effect on encouraging waste separation, as people might think 'I have to pay anyway, so I might as well throw everything in residual waste.' We prefer to try a pilot with payment per kilo of residual waste.

128354.explanation: LEF believes that with genuine participation and decisiveness, the park can be a good place. By making decisions together with fair operators and visitors, we ensure we bring the atmosphere and food trucks/dining spots to the park.

128357.explanation: We want more activities in Zwolle. A new event hall creates more connection and meeting opportunities.

128360.explanation: Youth care is an important part of supporting young people who need help for whatever reason. Every child has the right to a safe upbringing. It also helps prevent future problems. Zwolle must invest to improve youth care.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The residents of Zwolle must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zwolle moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Zwolle must introduce a parking-permit system in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Zwolle moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: Zwolle must abolish the pay-per-trash-bag system.
Zwolle must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: At least 40% of the new homes to be built in Zwolle must be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Zwolle moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Zwolle must (continue to) provide accommodation for rejected asylum seekers.

titleParts.text: Zwolle moet opvang (blijven) bieden aan afgewezen

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.title: Zwolle must introduce more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
In Zwolle the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves must remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Zwolle moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Zwolle must invest additional money in the new events hall.
Parking at shopping centers must remain free.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Business parks in Zwolle may be allowed to expand.
Zwolle must run a basic income pilot.

titleParts.text: Zwolle moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: The center of Zwolle must become car-free.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Zwolle must invest more money in youth care.

titleParts.text: Zwolle moet meer geld steken in

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

statements.title: Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
More high-rise apartment buildings may be allowed in Zwolle.
The summer fair must be moved from Park de Wezenlanden back to the city center.
